title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 4+ Palestinians in New Wave of Gaza Attacks
detail: A new wave of Israeli airstrikes hit the Gaza Strip on April 26, 2026, killing at least four Palestinians according to Gaza health officials and Middle East Monitor. The IDF stated one strike targeted militants operating a drone that posed a threat. The attacks continued a pattern of daily Israeli strikes that the UN and international rights groups have condemned as violations of the October 2025 ceasefire framework. The cumulative Palestinian death toll since October 7, 2023 rose to at least 72,587 according to the Gaza Ministry of Health, with 2 additional fatalities and 11 injuries recorded in the 24 hours prior to April 26 (WAFA).

media.caption: New wave of Israeli strikes kill 4 Palestinians in Gaza on April 26, 2026

title: Gaza Holds First Municipal Elections in Over 20 Years in Deir al-Balah
detail: Gaza held its first municipal elections in over 20 years on April 25–26, 2026, with voting limited to Deir al-Balah — the central Gaza city that has served as a humanitarian hub throughout the conflict. The vote represented a symbolic step toward governance amid the stalled Phase 2 ceasefire negotiations, which remain deadlocked over Hamas disarmament demands. Hamas has conditioned any disarmament discussion on a full Israeli withdrawal and recognition of Palestinian statehood. US Special Advisor Aryeh Lightstone met Hamas negotiator Khalil al-Hayya in Cairo the previous week as part of ongoing ceasefire-implementation talks. Al Jazeera noted on April 26 that the Gaza question faces growing uncertainty now that US diplomatic attention is increasingly focused on the Iran and Lebanon ceasefire deals.

media.caption: Gaza holds first elections in over 20 years as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled

title: UN Security Council Open Debate Calls to Consolidate Gaza Ceasefire, Halt Escalating Violence
detail: The UN Security Council held an open debate in late April 2026 focused on the Gaza ceasefire, during which the UN Deputy Special Coordinator addressed the Council and called on member states to consolidate the ceasefire, halt escalating violence, and address West Bank settlement expansion. The Council debate referenced the ongoing daily Israeli strikes as violating the ceasefire framework and called for Phase 2 negotiations to accelerate. Members also pressed Israel to allow unimpeded humanitarian access, noting that aid delivery — while improved in mid-April — remains at approximately 37% of required levels. The UN Secretary-General reiterated that 93% of the $4.1B 2026 Gaza humanitarian appeal remains unfunded.

media.caption: UN Security Council calls to consolidate Gaza ceasefire and halt escalating violence
